title: Preanesthesia: Brittany Cole, NPO Violation with MH Risk
description: Preanesthesia assessment of an elective knee arthroscopy with a recent heavy meal (ASA fasting violation), malignant-hyperthermia family history, and a predicted difficult airway. The correct disposition is to postpone; an instructor-only training branch exercises trigger-free RSI, difficult intubation, and MH recognition. DISCLOSURE: the MH timeline is compressed approximately 5x (EtCO2 and temperature rise faster than clinical reality); teach the SIGN ORDERING (EtCO2 rises before temperature) and that the real early rise is insidious, not the sim's fast pace. Clinical sourcing in docs/case-clinical-sourcing.md (MH from NAS 560 MH deck; fasting from ASA standards, operator-directed).
physiologyDisclosures: MH timeline compressed approximately 5x versus real-time: EtCO2 rises ~47->119 mmHg in the first minute and temperature ~1C/min versus the reference ~1C/5min. Sign ordering is correct and is the teaching point (EtCO2 early, temperature late). The real early rise is insidious and easy to miss; the sim's fast rise is a time-teaching compression. Engine MH math unchanged. See docs/airway-gaps-model.md simplification 11.
PONV, GERD, and smoking are not engine-modeled and are assessed as plan items only; the monitor does not react to them.
Difficult airway is intubation-side only (first attempt fails, mask rescue succeeds); difficult mask ventilation is not modeled.
learningObjectives: Recognize an inadequate fasting interval against ASA standards and postpone an elective case
Escalate a malignant-hyperthermia family history and plan a trigger-free anesthetic
Recognize that ASA physical status is not changed by an acute NPO violation
Prepare for a predicted difficult airway and, in the training branch, manage failed intubation and MH

patientProfile.comorbidities: MH family history; predicted difficult airway

debrief.summary: Elective knee arthroscopy with a fasting violation and MH family history; the correct disposition is to postpone.

sections.heading: HPI
sections.text: 26-year-old woman scheduled for elective right knee arthroscopy for a meniscal tear. Reports she ate a full breakfast about two hours before arrival because she misunderstood the fasting instructions.

sections.heading: Family history
sections.text: A maternal uncle had a severe reaction under general anesthesia with high fever and rigidity requiring intensive care; details are limited but the family was told it may run in the family.

actions.prompt: Ask about the last solid and liquid intake and confirm fasting status
actions.response: Ate a full breakfast (eggs, toast, sausage) about two hours ago; did not realize she needed to fast.

actions.prompt: Ask specifically whether any family member had an adverse reaction to anesthesia
actions.response: A maternal uncle had a dangerous high-fever, rigid reaction under anesthesia and nearly died; the family was warned it could be inherited.

actions.prompt: Address pregnancy status and last menstrual period
actions.response: Last menstrual period is uncertain; pregnancy status is not documented.

actions.prompt: Perform and document the airway exam
actions.response: Mallampati III, thyromental distance 5 cm, limited neck extension, mouth opening 3.5 cm. Predicts a difficult direct laryngoscopy.

actions.prompt: Examine and document dentition and injury risk
actions.response: A loose upper incisor and a chipped molar are noted; dental-injury risk must be documented before laryngoscopy.

findings.learnerLabel: Full meal about two hours ago
findings.significance: Violates the ASA fasting minimum for solids (six to eight hours). An elective case with a full stomach should be postponed.
findings.reviewNote: U-2: the specific fasting-interval threshold is ASA operator-directed, not in an attached course document. Confirm the standard/version.

findings.significance: A family history of a fever-and-rigidity anesthetic reaction demands MH escalation, a trigger-free plan (no succinylcholine, no volatile agents), and a diagnosis established before proceeding.
Pregnancy status and last menstrual period are required documentation for an elective case in a person of childbearing potential.
findings.reviewNote: U-4: the indication for a pregnancy test and the action on a positive result are not in an attached document. Confirm policy.

findings.significance: Mallampati III, short thyromental distance, and limited neck extension predict a difficult direct laryngoscopy; plan a rescue path.
findings.reviewNote: U-5: the specific airway exam values (Mallampati III, TMD 5 cm, etc.) are authored to the DOC-B field structure, not supplied for this patient. Confirm.

findings.significance: Loose and chipped teeth must be documented before laryngoscopy and the patient informed of injury risk.

rules.label: Keeps ASA physical status unchanged by the NPO violation
rules.reviewNote: U-6: 'ASA-PS is unchanged by an NPO violation' is ASA operator-directed and supported by analogy in DOC-A, not stated verbatim in an attached document. Confirm.

rules.label: Postpones the elective case for the fasting violation
Plans a trigger-free technique (no succinylcholine, no volatile)
Communicates the plan to the team

effect.description: Malignant hyperthermia unmasked by a triggering agent

branches.label: Instructor-only training counterfactual (NOT the recommended disposition)

considerations.title: Fasting violation
considerations.consideration: The trainee should elicit the recent full meal and recognize it violates the ASA fasting minimum for solids, making an elective case unsafe to proceed.
considerations.expectedResponse: Elicits the two-hour-old full breakfast and identifies inadequate fasting.
redFlags: Does not ask about last intake
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for eliciting the meal and naming the fasting violation.

considerations.title: MH family history
considerations.consideration: The trainee should specifically ask about family anesthetic reactions, recognize a probable malignant-hyperthermia history, and plan escalation with a trigger-free technique.
considerations.expectedResponse: Elicits the uncle's fever-and-rigidity reaction and plans MH escalation and trigger-free anesthesia.
redFlags: Plans succinylcholine or a volatile agent
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for eliciting the history and stating a trigger-free plan.

considerations.title: Difficult-airway exam
considerations.consideration: The airway exam predicts difficulty (Mallampati III, short thyromental distance, limited extension) and must be documented with a rescue plan.
considerations.expectedResponse: Documents the difficult-airway exam and plans a rescue path.
considerations.scoringGuidance: Credit for documenting the exam and predicting difficulty.

considerations.title: ASA is not changed by NPO
considerations.consideration: The trainee should classify ASA physical status by systemic disease. An acute fasting violation does not change the ASA class.
considerations.expectedResponse: Assigns ASA II and states the NPO violation does not change it.
redFlags: Raises the ASA class because of the full stomach
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for the correct ASA and the explicit reasoning.

considerations.title: Correct disposition
considerations.consideration: The correct disposition for an elective case with a fasting violation and an unresolved MH history is to postpone and optimize.
considerations.expectedResponse: Chooses to postpone and escalate rather than proceed.
redFlags: Proceeds with an elective full-stomach case
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for postponing; declining to proceed is the successful outcome.

considerations.title: Deferral is a success
considerations.consideration: Deferring this case is the correct, successful clinical decision and should be reinforced as such, not treated as a failure.
considerations.expectedResponse: Recognizes the deferral as the right call.

considerations.title: Training counterfactual
considerations.consideration: This branch is an instructor-selected educational counterfactual. Proceeding is NOT the recommended clinical disposition; it exists only to practice downstream management.
considerations.expectedResponse: Understands this is a teaching exercise, not the correct disposition.
considerations.scoringGuidance: State plainly that proceeding was wrong and this is practice only.

considerations.title: Unsafe trigger given
considerations.consideration: Giving succinylcholine (or a volatile agent) to an MH-susceptible patient is an unsafe trigger that can unmask malignant hyperthermia.
considerations.expectedResponse: Recognizes that succinylcholine is a trigger to be avoided.
redFlags: Gives succinylcholine to an MH-susceptible patient
considerations.scoringGuidance: Mark the trigger as unsafe and connect it to MH risk.

considerations.title: Trigger-free technique maintained
considerations.consideration: A trigger-free induction (propofol or etomidate, a non-depolarizing relaxant, no volatile) avoids unmasking MH.

considerations.title: Failed first attempt
considerations.consideration: The first intubation attempt fails as predicted; the trainee should limit attempts, maintain oxygenation, and move to a rescue plan.
considerations.expectedResponse: Recognizes the failed attempt and prioritizes oxygenation.
considerations.scoringGuidance: Credit for limiting attempts and oxygenating.

considerations.title: Rescue oxygenation
considerations.consideration: Mask ventilation between attempts restores oxygenation before a second, optimized attempt.
considerations.expectedResponse: Provides mask ventilation to recover saturation.

considerations.title: MH crisis recognition and treatment
considerations.consideration: Rising EtCO2 with tachycardia is the early sign of malignant hyperthermia; later, temperature climbs. Treat immediately: stop the trigger, hyperventilate with 100 percent oxygen, give dantrolene 2.5 mg per kg (repeat to effect), cool, and call for help (MHAUS).
considerations.expectedResponse: Recognizes rising EtCO2, stops the trigger, gives dantrolene 2.5 mg/kg, hyperventilates, and cools.
redFlags: Attributes the rising EtCO2 to hypoventilation only
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for early recognition and dantrolene plus supportive care.

considerations.title: Training emergence
considerations.consideration: After the training exercise, review the trigger-free emergence and the plan for MH monitoring in the ICU.
considerations.expectedResponse: Reviews continued MH monitoring and the recovery plan.

teachingItems.title: Fasting violation and postponement
teachingItems.explanation: A recent full meal violates the ASA fasting minimum for solids; an inappropriate fast is a recognized reason to delay or cancel an elective case. Source: ASA fasting standards (operator-directed) and NAS 530 assessment lecture.

teachingItems.title: Malignant hyperthermia susceptibility
teachingItems.explanation: A family history of a fever-and-rigidity anesthetic reaction requires escalation and a trigger-free technique. Volatile agents and succinylcholine are triggers; propofol, etomidate, opioids, benzodiazepines, ketamine, non-depolarizing relaxants, and local anesthetics are safe. Source: NAS 560 MH deck.

teachingItems.title: ASA status and NPO
teachingItems.explanation: ASA physical status reflects systemic disease and is not changed by an acute fasting violation. Source: ASA-PS standards (operator-directed).

teachingItems.title: Deferral as the correct decision
teachingItems.explanation: Declining to proceed with an unsafe elective case is a successful clinical decision, not a failure. Source: NAS 530 assessment lecture.

teachingItems.title: The training branch is a counterfactual
teachingItems.explanation: Proceeding here is an instructor-selected teaching exercise and is never the recommended disposition for this patient.

teachingItems.title: Trigger-free technique
teachingItems.explanation: Avoid succinylcholine and all volatile agents; use propofol or etomidate with a non-depolarizing relaxant and a clean machine. Source: NAS 560 MH deck.

teachingItems.title: Managing the difficult airway
teachingItems.explanation: Limit intubation attempts, oxygenate by mask between attempts, apply cricoid for the full stomach, and complete intubation in fewer than three attempts. Source: Carson-Newman RSI rubric.

teachingItems.title: Treating an MH crisis
teachingItems.explanation: Stop the trigger, hyperventilate with 100 percent oxygen, give dantrolene 2.5 mg/kg repeated to effect (up to 10 mg/kg), cool actively, correct acidosis, and monitor in the ICU for 6 to 72 hours. Source: NAS 560 MH deck.
